intro: Allocaryastrum gracile, also known as African potato, is a plant species that belongs to the Asphodelaceae family. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are said to help alleviate conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Allocaryastrum gracile include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
